headline: ECRL Cost Balloons to RM88B From Original RM55B
context: The East Coast Rail Link's revised cost estimate reached RM88 billion in 2025 including land acquisition and interest during construction, up 60% from the renegotiated RM55 billion price agreed with China Communications Construction Company.

cards.big: ECRL now costs RM88 billion — 60% above the 'renegotiated' price that was itself called a victory for Malaysia
cards.sub: Per-kilometre cost of RM133 million exceeds Japan's Shinkansen at RM115 million per km

cards.big: ECRL's economic return revised downward to 4.2% IRR — below government's own 8% threshold for mega-projects
cards.sub: Passenger demand projections of 8.4 million annually by 2035 assume 340% growth from current east coast travel volumes

cards.big: 85% of construction workforce is Chinese nationals per CCCC contract — Malaysian job creation well below original promises
cards.sub: Technology transfer provisions produced only 2 of 12 planned local supply chain partnerships after 4 years

cards.big: ECRL governance sits under MRL — a single-project company with no track record managing RM88 billion infrastructure
cards.sub: Parliamentary oversight limited to annual budget approval — no real-time cost monitoring or variance reporting

cards.big: Every billion over budget on ECRL is a billion not spent on the East Coast communities it claims to serve
Should Parliament establish a dedicated ECRL oversight committee with quarterly public cost and progress reporting?
